title: Single-source shortest paths on a non-negative-weighted directed graph

informal_statement.text:
Given a directed graph with non-negative integer edge weights, a source vertex, and a vertex count n, return a vector dist of length n where dist[v] is the length of the shortest path from source to v, or None if no such path exists.

Benchmark note (graph_dijkstra_004): Single-source shortest paths (variant 4): emphasize zero-weight edges and tie-breaking stability of distances.
Authoring lens: Lens 4 targets vacuous distance updates that forget to propagate through weight-0 stacks.
preconditions: every edge's endpoints are in [0, n)
required_properties: for every v, if dist[v] == Some(d), there exists a path from source to v of total weight d
for every v, if dist[v] == Some(d), no path from source to v has total weight less than d
for every v, if dist[v] == None, there is no path from source to v
